title: Brasilochloa sampaiana
intro: Brasilochloa sampaiana, also known as Sampaio's grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential antinociceptive, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Brasilochloa sampaiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, teas, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Brasilochloa sampaiana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Brasilochloa, which is a group of grasses that are native to South America. Brasilochloa sampaiana is classified as a monocot, meaning it has one cotyledon in its seedling stage. The species is further classified as an annual or perennial grass, depending on the specific characteristics of the plant. Brasilochloa sampaiana is closely related to other species of grass within the same genus.
